posts.title: Building tikho.me — a static blog with no framework
posts.excerpt: How this blog works: a static frontend on S3 + CloudFront, a small Python Lambda for likes and comments, and the fixes that made refreshes, routing and regression checks feel solid.

posts.title: Building a self-service platform on Kubernetes with Operators, Karpenter and Kyverno
posts.excerpt: A concept for building a self-service internal developer platform on Kubernetes — using a custom operator to manage projects and environments, Karpenter for on-demand node provisioning, and Kyverno to transparently handle pod scheduling without touching developer workloads.

posts.title: Success story: how my Upwork account was resumed after 4 years of suspension
posts.excerpt: In 2022 Upwork suspended my account. The support email was unmonitored, the appeal form gave nonsensical rejections, and live agents were gone. Here's what eventually worked — and what I'd tell anyone in the same situation.

posts.title: Running WordPress on AWS ECS Fargate
posts.excerpt: A practical Terraform setup for running WordPress on ECS Fargate with CloudFront, ALB, WAF, Aurora, EFS, SSM secrets, and SFTP uploads via AWS Transfer Family.

posts.title: Practical tips on cost saving with AWS for EKS-based platforms
posts.excerpt: Practical techniques for cutting the AWS bill on an EKS platform: Karpenter with Spot and consolidation, Graviton instances, PrivateLink, zonal routing to eliminate cross-AZ charges, gp3 EBS, CloudWatch log retention, and leveraging shared org services.
